headline: 3 of Wall Street’s Favorite Stocks with Open Questions
summary: Wall Street has set ambitious price targets for the stocks in this article. While this suggests attractive upside potential, it’s important to remain skeptical because analysts face institutional pressures that can sometimes lead to overly optimistic forecasts.

headline: Netflix Stock Has Lost Steam. This Next Step Could Heat Things Up Again.
summary: Netflix  is launching vertical videos to compete for engagement with social media companies, and this  J.P. Morgan  analyst thinks thats a solid plan.  Netflix  announced when reporting first-quarter earnings on April 16 that it will be launching vertical videos at the end of the month.  “We view vertical video as a way to engage users on another screen beyond the TV, and capture share of shorter, snackable moments, which could ultimately lead to increased engagement with NFLX’s longer-form content,” J.P. Morgan’s Doug Anmuth wrote in a research note.

headline: Disney announces limited-time, nostalgic retail experiences
summary: Almost 40 years ago, Disney opened its first retail store in California’s Glendale Galleria shopping mall. Before that 1987 launch, the company’s only owned retail sites were in its various parks, but as its fan base grew, executives decided stand-alone shopping experiences were worth a shot. By ...

headline: White House, UAE financial deal talk: Damage done by war to Mideast economy extends well beyond energy
summary: The United Arab Emirates has engineered one of the world's most resilient travel and tourism economies. This Mideast war may be its biggest rebound test.
